description.para: Map DSITxSS handler IDs to the underlying DSI core handler IDs.

briefdescription.para: Unsupported data type handler ID.
Pixel data underrun handler ID.
Command queue FIFO full handler ID.
Other error handler ID.

description.para: Mapped DSITxSS interrupt status masks from the DSI core.

briefdescription.para: Data ID error interrupt status mask.
Pixel underrun interrupt status mask.
Command queue FIFO full interrupt status mask.

description.para: Mapped DSITxSS interrupt enable masks from the DSI core.

briefdescription.para: Data ID error interrupt enable mask.
Pixel underrun interrupt enable mask.
Command queue FIFO full interrupt enable mask.
Enable all supported interrupts mask.
Disable DSI TX subsystem.
Enable DSI TX subsystem.
DSI TX Subsystem enable/disable selection enumeration.

detaileddescription.para: Specifies whether to enable or disable the DSI TX Subsystem core.

briefdescription.para: DSI Core.
DPHY Core.
DSI TX Subsystem sub-core selection enumeration.

detaileddescription.para: Specifies which sub-core (DSI or DPHY) within the subsystem to operate on.

briefdescription.para: Callback type for all interrupts defined.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XDSITXSS_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xdsitxss_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: DSI controller driver instance.
Global array of DSI TX Subsystem sub-core instances.

detaileddescription.para: Each array element contains the driver instances for all sub-cores (DSI, MIPI TX PHY, DPHY) associated with an instance of the DSI TX Subsystem.

briefdescription.para: Configuration table containing hardware configuration information for each XDsiTxSs instance in the system.

detaileddescription.para: This table is populated by the platform configuration tool and is referenced by XDsiTxSs_LookupConfig during device initialization.

briefdescription.para: This function queries the subsystem instance configuration to determine the included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: For each sub-core that is present in the design the sub-core driver instance is binded with the subsystem sub-core driver handle

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the DSI sub-core initialization.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on DSI sub core initialization

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the MIPI DSI TX subsystem and included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the subsystem. Initialization includes setting up the instance data for top level as well as all included sub-core therein, and ensuring the hardware is in a known stable state

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
points to the configuration structure associated with the subsystem instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to configure the DSI default parameters that are to be handled by the application.

detaileddescription.para: It will configure protocol register with video mode, bllp mode,eotp

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful configuration of parameters

briefdescription.para: This function is used to activate the DSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it activates the DPHY and DSI. Enable/Disable IP core to start processing

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is used to denote the subcore of subsystem
is used to denote whether to enable or disable the subsystem

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS is returned if subcore(DSI/DPHY) was successfully enabled or disabled XST_INVALID_PARAM is returned if subsystem core is not found

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the DSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it resets the DPHY and DSI

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function reports list of cores included in DSI TX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI TX Subsystem instance.

listitem.para: This function sets the mode to send short packet.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxSs instance to operate on
is the DSI mode (video or command) to operate on

listitem.para: This function will send the short packet to controller in command mode
Generic Short Packet Register and fill up the structure passed from caller.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxSs instance to operate on

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE on failure in packet transmission

briefdescription.para: This function will send the short packet to controller Generic Short Packet Register and fill up the structure passed from caller.

detaileddescription.para: like to turn on/off peripheral, change color mode

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxSs instance to operate on

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information from the GUI settings.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information from the GUI settings.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxss instance to operate on

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get pixel format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DsiTxSs Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0x0E – Packed RGB565 0x1E- packed RGB666 0x2E – Loosely packed RGB666 0x3E- Packed RGB888 0x0B- Compressed Pixel Stream

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get Command queue Vacancy.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSITxSs Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Number of command queue entries can be safely written to Command queue FIFO, before it goes full.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDsiTxSs *InstancePtr, XDsi_VideoMode VideoMode, XVidC_VideoMode Resolution, u16 BurstPacketSize)
memberdef.name: XDsiTxSs_SetVideoInterfaceTiming

briefdescription.para: This function Set Timing mode and Resolution.

detaileddescription.para: As per user resolution selection it will get populate Periperal Timing Parameters from video common Library

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on
Specifies mode of Interfacing
sets the resolution
sets the packet size

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS is return Video interfacing set was successful
XST_INVALID_PARAM indicates an invalid parameter was specified.

briefdescription.para: This function Set Timning mode and Resolution as per user inputs.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on
Specifies mode of Interfacing
Video Timing parameters

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS is return Video interfacing was successfully set
XST_INVALID_PARAM indicates an invalid parameter was specified.

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XDsiTxSs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each instance of the device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Index number of config table using BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the instance structure
address of the instance

simplesect.para: Index number of the config table

briefdescription.para: This function performs self test on MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDsiTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XDSI_HANDLER_UNSUPPORT_DATATYPE Un support data type detected XDSI_HANDLER_PIXELDATA_UNDERRUN Byte Stream FIFO starves for Pixel during HACT transmission XDSI_HANDLER_OTHERERROR Any other type of interrupt has occurred like Stream Line Buffer Full, Incorrect Lanes, etc XDSI_HANDLER_CMDQ_FIFOFULL Command queue FIFO Full

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallbackFunc

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XDsiTxSs_SetCallback() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDsiTxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a value from a MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XDsiTxSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

briefdescription.para: This function writes a value to a MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XDsiTxSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

briefdescription.para: This function will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxSs instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: This function will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsiTxSs instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
This is main code of Xilinx MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem device driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem driver's static initialization functionality.
This file contains self test function for the MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem.
This is the interrupt handling part of the Xilinx MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem device driver.
This header file contains identifiers and register-level core functions (or macros) that can be used to access the Xilinx MIPI DSI Tx Subsystem core.
Please see xdsitxss.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 11/02/16 Initial Release for MIPI DSI TX subsystem 1.1 sss 08/17/16 Added 64 bit support sss 08/26/16 Add "Command Queue Vacancy" API API for getting pixel format

detaileddescription.para: For more information about the operation of this core see the hardware specification and documentation in the higher level driver xdsitxss.h file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 11/02/16 Initial Release for MIPI DSI TX subsystem 1.1 sss 08/17/16 Added 64 bit support sss 08/26/16 Added "Command Queue Vacancy FIFO Full" interrupt support

detaileddescription.para: The interrupt registration and handler are defined here. The callbacks are registered for events which are interrupts clubbed together on the basis of the DSI specification. Refer to DSI driver for the event groups.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 11/02/16 Initial Release for MIPI DSI TX subsystem 1.1 sss 08/26/16 Added "Command Queue Vacancy FIFO Full" interrupt support

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 11/2/16 Initial Release for MIPI DSI TX subsystem
